content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Both amendments identify real regressions in same-bead project resolution. One turns an otherwise recoverable all-terminal retry history into a hard milestone-run failure, and the other lets a legacy default-id heuristic misclassify unrelated manual projects as same-bead duplicates during create-time validation.

## Votes

- `fr-12-c8877f63`: Accept
  `find_existing_bead_project` now errors when more than one matching same-bead project is `Completed`, while `reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation` explicitly treats that same all-terminal state as safe for fresh creation. Because `ensure_project_for_controller` calls `find_existing_bead_project` before it can create or recover anything, a controller that lost `active_task_id` cannot progress through a completed-default-plus-completed-retry history even though there is no live same-bead work to protect. That is a real correctness/regression issue in the recovery path, not just a policy difference.
- `fr-12-eee3108c`: Accept
  The create-time duplicate guard is using `matching_bead_project_ids`, and that helper's fallback treats any task-source-free project whose id matches the bead's generated default id as a same-bead project. In `reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation`, that inferred match is enough to block `task create` and emit resume/select guidance, even when the project is just an unrelated manual project with a colliding name. Since the blocker fires without actual milestone/bead lineage evidence, it creates false positives and makes bead creation behavior depend on unrelated project naming, which is a real correctness and maintainability defect.
